seoTitle: Set Up Short and Long Answer Questions in Forms
metaDescription: Configure text-based questions in Microsoft Forms, switch between short and long answer formats, and restrict input to numbers or character limits.
intro: Open-ended text questions need the right format to capture useful data, whether that's a single-line email address or a full paragraph of essay feedback. This guide covers setting up short and long answer text fields in Microsoft Forms and applying restrictions so responses stay clean.

steps.heading: Add a Text question
steps.body: Click Add New and select the Text question type from the field menu.

steps.heading: Write your prompt
steps.body: Click into the question text zone and enter your descriptive open-ended prompt.

steps.heading: Switch to long answer if needed
steps.body: By default the field is a single-line short answer box — slide the Long Answer toggle on to deploy an expanded multi-line box instead.

steps.heading: Add restrictions and a subtitle
steps.body: Use the three-dot More Settings menu to add a subtitle with instructions, or apply restrictions that limit input to specific numeric ranges or character lengths.

steps.heading: Require the field and preview
steps.body: Toggle Required if the question shouldn't be skippable, then use Preview to test typing limits on both desktop and mobile.

faq.question: When should I use short answer instead of long answer?
faq.answer: Short answer suits brief entries like names or emails, while long answer fits detailed feedback or essay-style responses.

faq.question: Can I restrict a text field to only accept numbers?
faq.answer: Yes, the Restrictions option under the three-dot menu lets you limit entries to numeric ranges or specific digit counts.

faq.question: How do I give respondents formatting instructions?
faq.answer: Use the Subtitle option to add clear guidance directly below the question prompt.

faq.question: Can I require an answer before the form can be submitted?
faq.answer: Yes, toggling the Required switch prevents blank submissions for that specific text question.
